lyrics.text: File 82-712. Agent Rick Dicker interrogating.
State your name, please.
Uh, Tony. Tony Rydinger.
Tell me about the incident.
There's this girl in my class. I saw her at the track meeting.
You're uh Violet, right? That's me. I sort of knew her.
But she changed. Relaxed. She was more sure of herself. Cool. Cute.
I asked her out to a movie. So Friday. Friday. Friday.
And I went to watch the track meet.
Not long after, this thing happened in the parking lot.
Behold the Underminer!
I am always beneath you.
As if things aren't weird enough. I look over and I see these boots.
Wait, should we be doing this? It is still illegal.
They look like superheroes. We're going to lose it.
One of you patrol the perimeter. Keep the crowds back and safe.
The other, watch after Jack-Jack. But I thought we were going to go.
You heard your mother. Trampoline me.
I got perimeter. You're not going anywhere.
This is a magic crate. I see my chance to get out of there.
There's something familiar about one of the kids' voices.
The girl, she's upset. She pulled down her mask and it's her.
Oh, Tony. Hi. Uh, this isn't what you think.
It was just getting too freaky. I couldn't handle it and ran off.
I feel kind of bad about it. Maybe I should have said hi or something.
It's not her fault superheroes are illegal.
And it's not like I don't like strong girls. I'm pretty secure manhood wise.
What is that? Have you told anyone else about this? Your parents?
No, they'd only think I was hiding something. You know what I mean?
Sure, kid. I like this girl, Mr. Dicker.
I'm supposed to go out with her Friday night. Now things are just going to be weird.
I wish I could forget I ever saw her in that suit.
You will, kid. You will.
